name: battlefield-recent
summary: Ground where a battle ended hours or days ago. Corpses not yet cleared, banners half-trampled, weapons driven into the mud. Crows are the only inhabitants. A place to loot, identify the dead, or ambush survivors.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: broad and open — battles don't happen in corridors

accent.purpose: churned mud or charred patch where a spell landed

primary_palette.note: wrecked trebuchet or ballista as landmark
rally point where the line broke
the clean-up has begun at the center
dead officer beside dead horse — a recognizable heraldic centerpiece

story_prompts: hours after — the dying still moan, the living haven't returned yet
a looter cart works the field; the party can beat them to a dead officer
necromancer drawn to the carrion is raising a small undead warband
two days after — bodies are bloated, crows everywhere, clean-up crew arriving
ambush — the losing side left survivors hiding under corpses

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: outdoor overcast (0.4-0.55) with drained color — tragic, grey

anti_patterns: invisible walls (iw) at the perimeter — the field continues out of frame
don't arrange corpses in rows — bodies fall in patterns of rout and clumping
mud and blood fills should be common — clean grass is wrong here
never forget the crows — scatter birds liberally, they are the ambient life
